title: Asperula gobica

Asperula, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of digestive issues and as a natural remedy for sore throats and mouth ulcers. The bioactive constituents of Asperula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthraquinones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Asperula include teas and infusions, as well as dried extracts and tinctures.
